behaviors.reasoning: Signature verification requires the real HMAC module wired to the real request handler — testing the chain from raw body to rejection.
Queue-before-ack ordering requires the real handler wired to the real persistent queue to verify write happens before response.
Async decoupling — handler returns without waiting for downstream. Needs real modules wired (handler + queue + downstream stub) but no real network.
3-second wall-clock guarantee under concurrent load requires real async execution with real timing — can't be mocked.
Dedup requires the real queue (or equivalent) to verify idempotency across the handler→queue boundary.
Pure conditional: if isFull() then return 503 with header. The decision logic is a single function boundary, no wiring needed.
Capacity enforcement requires the real persistent queue to fill up and reject — the queue's internal state management is the boundary.
